title: Grade 4 Math
description: STAAR Grade 4 Math curriculum aligned to the Texas Essential Knowledge and Skills (TEKS). Topics are organized in teaching order from place value through personal financial literacy.

units.title: Place Value to 1,000,000,000
units.summary: Read, write, compare, and round whole numbers up to one billion.

lessons.title: Place value to one billion
lessons.objective: Represent the value of digits in whole numbers through 1,000,000,000.

questions.prompt: What is the value of the 6 in 4,628,135?
questions.explanation: The 6 sits in the hundred-thousands place: 6 × 100,000 = 600,000.

questions.prompt: Write 305,072 in expanded form's hundred-thousands value.
questions.explanation: The 3 is in the hundred-thousands place, so its value is 300,000.

questions.prompt: What is the value of the digit 8 in 78,166,788?
questions.explanation: The 8 sits in the millions place: 8 × 1,000,000 = 8,000,000.

questions.prompt: What is the value of the digit 8 in 5,806,854?
questions.explanation: The 8 sits in the hundred-thousands place: 8 × 100,000 = 800,000.

questions.prompt: What is the value of the digit 7 in 37,613?
questions.explanation: The 7 sits in the thousands place: 7 × 1,000 = 7,000.

questions.prompt: What is the value of the digit 6 in 56,906,030?
questions.explanation: The 6 sits in the millions place: 6 × 1,000,000 = 6,000,000.

questions.prompt: What is the value of the digit 9 in 19?
questions.explanation: The 9 sits in the ones place: 9 × 1 = 9.

questions.prompt: What is the value of the digit 7 in 67,907,454?
questions.explanation: The 7 sits in the millions place: 7 × 1,000,000 = 7,000,000.

questions.prompt: What is the value of the digit 8 in 48?
questions.explanation: The 8 sits in the ones place: 8 × 1 = 8.

questions.explanation: The 1 sits in the ones place: 1 × 1 = 1.

lessons.title: Comparing & ordering
lessons.objective: Compare and order whole numbers to 1,000,000,000 using place value.

questions.prompt: Which list is in order from least to greatest?
questions.explanation: All three share 412, so compare the last three digits: 008 < 080 < 800.

lessons.title: Rounding to any place
lessons.objective: Round whole numbers to a given place.

questions.prompt: Round 36,847 to the nearest thousand.
questions.explanation: The hundreds digit is 8, which rounds the thousands digit up from 6 to 7.

questions.prompt: Round 862 to the nearest ten.
questions.explanation: Look at the digit just right of the tens place. 862 rounds to 860.

units.title: Decimals to Hundredths
units.summary: Represent, compare, and relate decimals to hundredths and to fractions.

lessons.title: Decimal place value
lessons.objective: Represent decimals to the hundredths using money and concrete models.

questions.prompt: Which decimal is the same as 7 tenths and 3 hundredths?
questions.explanation: 7 tenths = 0.7, 3 hundredths = 0.03, total = 0.73.

questions.prompt: What digit is in the tenths place of 32.88?
questions.explanation: The first digit after the decimal point is tenths; the second is hundredths. In 32.88, the tenths digit is 8.

questions.prompt: What digit is in the tenths place of 22.21?
questions.explanation: The first digit after the decimal point is tenths; the second is hundredths. In 22.21, the tenths digit is 2.

lessons.title: Comparing decimals
lessons.objective: Compare and order decimals to hundredths using >, <, =.

questions.prompt: Which symbol makes this true?  0.4 ___ 0.39
questions.explanation: 0.4 = 0.40, and 0.40 > 0.39 because 40 hundredths is more than 39 hundredths.

questions.prompt: Which symbol makes this true?  1.11 ___ 1.64
questions.explanation: Line up the decimal points and compare digit by digit. 1.11 < 1.64.

questions.prompt: Which symbol makes this true?  7.69 ___ 5.71
questions.explanation: Line up the decimal points and compare digit by digit. 7.69 > 5.71.

lessons.title: Fractions and decimals
lessons.objective: Relate decimals to tenths and hundredths to fractions.

questions.prompt: Which fraction equals 0.45?
questions.explanation: 0.45 means 45 hundredths, written 45/100.

units.title: Fractions: Equivalence & Comparison
units.summary: Build, compare, and decompose fractions and mixed numbers.

lessons.title: Equivalent fractions
lessons.objective: Find equivalent fractions using multiplication or division.

questions.prompt: Which fraction is equivalent to 3/4?
questions.explanation: Multiply numerator and denominator by 3: 3×3/4×3 = 9/12.

lessons.title: Comparing fractions
lessons.objective: Compare two fractions with different numerators and denominators.

questions.prompt: Which fraction is greater than 2/3?

lessons.title: Decomposing fractions
lessons.objective: Decompose a fraction into a sum of unit fractions or simpler fractions.

questions.prompt: Which sum equals 5/8?
questions.explanation: 3/8 + 2/8 = 5/8. The other options sum to 3/8, 4/8, and 2/4.

units.title: Adding & Subtracting Fractions
units.summary: Add and subtract fractions with like denominators using objects, pictures, and properties.

lessons.title: Adding fractions (like denominators)
lessons.objective: Represent and solve addition of fractions with equal denominators.

questions.prompt: What is 3/8 + 2/8?
questions.explanation: Add the numerators and keep the denominator: 3+2 = 5, so 5/8.
Same denominator: add the numerators only. 4 + 1 = 5, so the answer is 5/6.
Same denominator: add the numerators only. 1 + 2 = 3, so the answer is 3/6.
Same denominator: add the numerators only. 4 + 3 = 7, so the answer is 7/11.
Same denominator: add the numerators only. 6 + 1 = 7, so the answer is 7/8.
Same denominator: add the numerators only. 7 + 1 = 8, so the answer is 8/10.
Same denominator: add the numerators only. 4 + 6 = 10, so the answer is 10/12.
Same denominator: add the numerators only. 2 + 4 = 6, so the answer is 6/9.
Same denominator: add the numerators only. 1 + 8 = 9, so the answer is 9/10.
Same denominator: add the numerators only. 1 + 1 = 2, so the answer is 2/4.
Same denominator: add the numerators only. 1 + 3 = 4, so the answer is 4/5.
Same denominator: add the numerators only. 2 + 1 = 3, so the answer is 3/4.
Same denominator: add the numerators only. 4 + 3 = 7, so the answer is 7/10.
Same denominator: add the numerators only. 3 + 1 = 4, so the answer is 4/5.
Same denominator: add the numerators only. 2 + 2 = 4, so the answer is 4/6.
Same denominator: add the numerators only. 2 + 7 = 9, so the answer is 9/12.
Same denominator: add the numerators only. 1 + 3 = 4, so the answer is 4/8.
Same denominator: add the numerators only. 4 + 1 = 5, so the answer is 5/10.
Same denominator: add the numerators only. 2 + 6 = 8, so the answer is 8/10.
Same denominator: add the numerators only. 1 + 2 = 3, so the answer is 3/7.
Same denominator: add the numerators only. 2 + 6 = 8, so the answer is 8/11.
Same denominator: add the numerators only. 5 + 1 = 6, so the answer is 6/10.

lessons.title: Subtracting fractions (like denominators)
lessons.objective: Subtract fractions with the same denominator.

questions.explanation: Subtract the numerators: 7−3 = 4. Keep the denominator: 4/10.
Same denominator: subtract numerators. 2 − 1 = 1, so the answer is 1/10.
Same denominator: subtract numerators. 3 − 1 = 2, so the answer is 2/4.
Same denominator: subtract numerators. 2 − 1 = 1, so the answer is 1/4.
Same denominator: subtract numerators. 7 − 3 = 4, so the answer is 4/8.
Same denominator: subtract numerators. 3 − 2 = 1, so the answer is 1/5.
Same denominator: subtract numerators. 2 − 1 = 1, so the answer is 1/5.
Same denominator: subtract numerators. 7 − 2 = 5, so the answer is 5/9.
Same denominator: subtract numerators. 3 − 2 = 1, so the answer is 1/4.
Same denominator: subtract numerators. 8 − 7 = 1, so the answer is 1/11.
Same denominator: subtract numerators. 5 − 2 = 3, so the answer is 3/8.
Same denominator: subtract numerators. 4 − 3 = 1, so the answer is 1/5.
Same denominator: subtract numerators. 7 − 1 = 6, so the answer is 6/12.
Same denominator: subtract numerators. 3 − 1 = 2, so the answer is 2/5.
Same denominator: subtract numerators. 3 − 2 = 1, so the answer is 1/6.
Same denominator: subtract numerators. 10 − 1 = 9, so the answer is 9/11.
Same denominator: subtract numerators. 2 − 1 = 1, so the answer is 1/8.
Same denominator: subtract numerators. 4 − 2 = 2, so the answer is 2/7.
Same denominator: subtract numerators. 6 − 5 = 1, so the answer is 1/8.
Same denominator: subtract numerators. 3 − 1 = 2, so the answer is 2/6.
Same denominator: subtract numerators. 7 − 3 = 4, so the answer is 4/11.
Same denominator: subtract numerators. 4 − 1 = 3, so the answer is 3/8.
Same denominator: subtract numerators. 4 − 2 = 2, so the answer is 2/12.
Same denominator: subtract numerators. 5 − 2 = 3, so the answer is 3/10.

units.title: Multi-Digit Multiplication
units.summary: Multiply up to a 4-digit by 1-digit and 2-digit by 2-digit numbers.

lessons.title: Multiplying by 1-digit
lessons.objective: Use the standard algorithm to multiply by a 1-digit number.

questions.prompt: What is 1,247 × 6?
questions.explanation: 6×7=42 (write 2 carry 4), 6×4+4=28 (write 8 carry 2), 6×2+2=14 (write 4 carry 1), 6×1+1=7.
Use the standard algorithm or partial products: 317 × 8 = 2,536.
Use the standard algorithm or partial products: 212 × 9 = 1,908.

lessons.title: Multiplying by 2-digit
lessons.objective: Multiply two 2-digit numbers using the standard algorithm.

questions.prompt: What is 38 × 24?

questions.explanation: Multiply by tens, then ones, and add: 55 × 59 = 3,245.
Multiply by tens, then ones, and add: 82 × 41 = 3,362.

lessons.title: Multiplication word problems
lessons.objective: Solve real-world multiplication problems.

questions.prompt: A theater has 28 rows with 16 seats in each row. How many seats are there?

units.title: Division
units.summary: Divide up to 4-digit dividends by 1-digit divisors with and without remainders.

lessons.title: Long division basics
lessons.objective: Use the standard algorithm to divide.

questions.prompt: What is 392 ÷ 7?

lessons.title: Division with remainders
lessons.objective: Interpret remainders in division situations.

questions.prompt: There are 47 stickers shared equally among 5 students. How many stickers are left over?
questions.explanation: 47 ÷ 5 = 9 remainder 2. Each student gets 9 stickers and 2 are left.

questions.prompt: What is the remainder when 125 is divided by 3?
questions.explanation: 125 ÷ 3 = 41 remainder 2, because 3 × 41 + 2 = 125.

questions.prompt: What is the remainder when 562 is divided by 6?
questions.explanation: 562 ÷ 6 = 93 remainder 4, because 6 × 93 + 4 = 562.

questions.prompt: What is the remainder when 667 is divided by 9?
questions.explanation: 667 ÷ 9 = 74 remainder 1, because 9 × 74 + 1 = 667.

questions.prompt: What is the remainder when 172 is divided by 3?
questions.explanation: 172 ÷ 3 = 57 remainder 1, because 3 × 57 + 1 = 172.

questions.prompt: What is the remainder when 188 is divided by 9?
questions.explanation: 188 ÷ 9 = 20 remainder 8, because 9 × 20 + 8 = 188.

questions.prompt: What is the remainder when 83 is divided by 3?
questions.explanation: 83 ÷ 3 = 27 remainder 2, because 3 × 27 + 2 = 83.

questions.prompt: What is the remainder when 271 is divided by 3?
questions.explanation: 271 ÷ 3 = 90 remainder 1, because 3 × 90 + 1 = 271.

questions.prompt: What is the remainder when 462 is divided by 5?
questions.explanation: 462 ÷ 5 = 92 remainder 2, because 5 × 92 + 2 = 462.

questions.prompt: What is the remainder when 728 is divided by 9?
questions.explanation: 728 ÷ 9 = 80 remainder 8, because 9 × 80 + 8 = 728.

questions.prompt: What is the remainder when 335 is divided by 4?
questions.explanation: 335 ÷ 4 = 83 remainder 3, because 4 × 83 + 3 = 335.

questions.prompt: What is the remainder when 122 is divided by 3?
questions.explanation: 122 ÷ 3 = 40 remainder 2, because 3 × 40 + 2 = 122.

questions.prompt: What is the remainder when 63 is divided by 5?
questions.explanation: 63 ÷ 5 = 12 remainder 3, because 5 × 12 + 3 = 63.

questions.prompt: What is the remainder when 163 is divided by 6?
questions.explanation: 163 ÷ 6 = 27 remainder 1, because 6 × 27 + 1 = 163.

questions.prompt: What is the remainder when 426 is divided by 8?
questions.explanation: 426 ÷ 8 = 53 remainder 2, because 8 × 53 + 2 = 426.

questions.prompt: What is the remainder when 181 is divided by 4?
questions.explanation: 181 ÷ 4 = 45 remainder 1, because 4 × 45 + 1 = 181.

questions.prompt: What is the remainder when 621 is divided by 8?
questions.explanation: 621 ÷ 8 = 77 remainder 5, because 8 × 77 + 5 = 621.

questions.prompt: What is the remainder when 421 is divided by 9?
questions.explanation: 421 ÷ 9 = 46 remainder 7, because 9 × 46 + 7 = 421.

questions.prompt: What is the remainder when 308 is divided by 8?
questions.explanation: 308 ÷ 8 = 38 remainder 4, because 8 × 38 + 4 = 308.

questions.prompt: What is the remainder when 77 is divided by 5?
questions.explanation: 77 ÷ 5 = 15 remainder 2, because 5 × 15 + 2 = 77.

questions.prompt: What is the remainder when 196 is divided by 5?
questions.explanation: 196 ÷ 5 = 39 remainder 1, because 5 × 39 + 1 = 196.

questions.prompt: What is the remainder when 262 is divided by 3?
questions.explanation: 262 ÷ 3 = 87 remainder 1, because 3 × 87 + 1 = 262.

questions.prompt: What is the remainder when 350 is divided by 6?
questions.explanation: 350 ÷ 6 = 58 remainder 2, because 6 × 58 + 2 = 350.

questions.prompt: What is the remainder when 188 is divided by 3?
questions.explanation: 188 ÷ 3 = 62 remainder 2, because 3 × 62 + 2 = 188.

questions.prompt: What is the remainder when 703 is divided by 9?
questions.explanation: 703 ÷ 9 = 78 remainder 1, because 9 × 78 + 1 = 703.

questions.prompt: What is the remainder when 471 is divided by 9?
questions.explanation: 471 ÷ 9 = 52 remainder 3, because 9 × 52 + 3 = 471.

units.title: Algebraic Patterns & Input-Output
units.summary: Represent multi-step problems with equations and identify number patterns.

lessons.title: Input-output tables
lessons.objective: Find a rule for input-output tables.

questions.prompt: If the rule is multiply by 4 then add 1, what is the output for input 6?

lessons.title: Multi-step equations
lessons.objective: Write an equation with a letter for the unknown.

questions.prompt: Maria bought 3 books for $8 each and a magazine for $4. Which equation finds her total t?
questions.explanation: 3 books at $8 = 3 × 8, then add the $4 magazine.

units.title: Geometry: Lines, Angles & Shapes
units.summary: Identify lines, classify angles, and measure with a protractor.

lessons.title: Lines & line segments
lessons.objective: Identify points, lines, line segments, rays, and parallel/perpendicular lines.

questions.prompt: Two lines that never cross and stay the same distance apart are called…
questions.explanation: Parallel lines never meet.

lessons.title: Classifying angles
lessons.objective: Identify acute, right, and obtuse angles.

questions.prompt: An angle that measures 130° is…
questions.explanation: Obtuse angles are between 90° and 180°.

questions.prompt: An angle measures 166°. What type of angle is it?
questions.explanation: Acute < 90°, right = 90°, obtuse between 90° and 180°, straight = 180°. 166° is obtuse.

questions.prompt: An angle measures 37°. What type of angle is it?
questions.explanation: Acute < 90°, right = 90°, obtuse between 90° and 180°, straight = 180°. 37° is acute.

questions.prompt: An angle measures 90°. What type of angle is it?
questions.explanation: Acute < 90°, right = 90°, obtuse between 90° and 180°, straight = 180°. 90° is right.

questions.prompt: An angle measures 96°. What type of angle is it?
questions.explanation: Acute < 90°, right = 90°, obtuse between 90° and 180°, straight = 180°. 96° is obtuse.

questions.prompt: An angle measures 111°. What type of angle is it?
questions.explanation: Acute < 90°, right = 90°, obtuse between 90° and 180°, straight = 180°. 111° is obtuse.

questions.prompt: An angle measures 161°. What type of angle is it?
questions.explanation: Acute < 90°, right = 90°, obtuse between 90° and 180°, straight = 180°. 161° is obtuse.

questions.prompt: An angle measures 40°. What type of angle is it?
questions.explanation: Acute < 90°, right = 90°, obtuse between 90° and 180°, straight = 180°. 40° is acute.

questions.prompt: An angle measures 138°. What type of angle is it?
questions.explanation: Acute < 90°, right = 90°, obtuse between 90° and 180°, straight = 180°. 138° is obtuse.

questions.prompt: An angle measures 159°. What type of angle is it?
questions.explanation: Acute < 90°, right = 90°, obtuse between 90° and 180°, straight = 180°. 159° is obtuse.

questions.prompt: An angle measures 48°. What type of angle is it?
questions.explanation: Acute < 90°, right = 90°, obtuse between 90° and 180°, straight = 180°. 48° is acute.

questions.prompt: An angle measures 57°. What type of angle is it?
questions.explanation: Acute < 90°, right = 90°, obtuse between 90° and 180°, straight = 180°. 57° is acute.

questions.prompt: An angle measures 18°. What type of angle is it?
questions.explanation: Acute < 90°, right = 90°, obtuse between 90° and 180°, straight = 180°. 18° is acute.

questions.prompt: An angle measures 142°. What type of angle is it?
questions.explanation: Acute < 90°, right = 90°, obtuse between 90° and 180°, straight = 180°. 142° is obtuse.

questions.prompt: An angle measures 76°. What type of angle is it?
questions.explanation: Acute < 90°, right = 90°, obtuse between 90° and 180°, straight = 180°. 76° is acute.

questions.prompt: An angle measures 128°. What type of angle is it?
questions.explanation: Acute < 90°, right = 90°, obtuse between 90° and 180°, straight = 180°. 128° is obtuse.

questions.prompt: An angle measures 45°. What type of angle is it?
questions.explanation: Acute < 90°, right = 90°, obtuse between 90° and 180°, straight = 180°. 45° is acute.

questions.prompt: An angle measures 106°. What type of angle is it?
questions.explanation: Acute < 90°, right = 90°, obtuse between 90° and 180°, straight = 180°. 106° is obtuse.

questions.prompt: An angle measures 64°. What type of angle is it?
questions.explanation: Acute < 90°, right = 90°, obtuse between 90° and 180°, straight = 180°. 64° is acute.

questions.prompt: An angle measures 100°. What type of angle is it?
questions.explanation: Acute < 90°, right = 90°, obtuse between 90° and 180°, straight = 180°. 100° is obtuse.

lessons.title: Measuring with a protractor
lessons.objective: Measure angles with a protractor.

questions.prompt: An angle is half of a straight angle. How many degrees is it?
questions.explanation: A straight angle is 180°, so half is 90°.

units.title: Perimeter & Area

lessons.title: Area of rectangles
lessons.objective: Calculate area using A = L × W.

questions.prompt: A rectangle has length 12 ft and width 5 ft. What is its area in square feet?
questions.explanation: A = 12 × 5 = 60 square feet.

questions.prompt: What is the area of a rectangle that is 19 m by 27 m?
questions.explanation: A = l × w = 19 × 27 = 513 square meters.

questions.prompt: What is the area of a rectangle that is 17 m by 26 m?
questions.explanation: A = l × w = 17 × 26 = 442 square meters.

questions.prompt: What is the area of a rectangle that is 15 m by 15 m?
questions.explanation: A = l × w = 15 × 15 = 225 square meters.

questions.prompt: What is the area of a rectangle that is 15 m by 13 m?
questions.explanation: A = l × w = 15 × 13 = 195 square meters.

questions.explanation: A = l × w = 10 × 28 = 280 square meters.

questions.explanation: A = l × w = 28 × 18 = 504 square meters.

questions.explanation: A = l × w = 12 × 10 = 120 square meters.

questions.explanation: A = l × w = 10 × 7 = 70 square meters.

questions.explanation: A = l × w = 7 × 12 = 84 square meters.

questions.prompt: What is the area of a rectangle that is 23 m by 15 m?

questions.prompt: What is the area of a rectangle that is 15 m by 8 m?
questions.explanation: A = l × w = 15 × 8 = 120 square meters.

units.title: Measurement Conversions
units.summary: Convert between units of length, weight, capacity, and time.

lessons.title: Customary length
lessons.objective: Convert between inches, feet, and yards.

questions.prompt: How many inches are in 4 feet?
questions.explanation: 1 foot = 12 inches, so 4 × 12 = 48 inches.

lessons.title: Time intervals
lessons.objective: Solve problems involving elapsed time.

questions.prompt: A movie starts at 1:45 p.m. and ends at 3:30 p.m. How long is the movie?
questions.explanation: From 1:45 to 3:00 is 1 h 15 min, plus 30 more min = 1 h 45 min.

units.title: Data Analysis
units.reportingCategory: Data Analysis and Personal Financial Literacy
units.summary: Represent and interpret data with frequency tables, dot plots, and stem-and-leaf plots.

lessons.title: Reading dot plots
lessons.objective: Interpret data displayed on a dot plot.

questions.prompt: On a dot plot of test scores, 4 dots stack at 90 and 2 dots stack at 100. How many students scored 90 or higher?
questions.explanation: Add the dots at 90 and at 100: 4 + 2 = 6 students.

units.title: Personal Financial Literacy
units.reportingCategory: Data Analysis and Personal Financial Literacy
units.summary: Distinguish income from expenses, fixed from variable expenses, and learn about saving and basic banking.

lessons.title: Fixed vs variable expenses
lessons.objective: Differentiate between fixed and variable expenses.

questions.prompt: Which is the BEST example of a fixed expense?
questions.explanation: Rent is the same amount each month, so it is a fixed expense.

lessons.title: Profit & income
lessons.objective: Describe profit as the result of subtracting expenses from income.

questions.prompt: A lemonade stand earns $42 and spends $15 on supplies. What is the profit in dollars?
questions.explanation: Profit = Income − Expenses = 42 − 15 = $27.
